# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official golang image from docker hub
FROM golang:1.16-alpine

# Add Maintainer info
LABEL maintainer="your_email@domain.com"

# Working directory in the container for compiling and installing our app 
WORKDIR /app

# Copy go mod and sum files 
COPY go.mod go.sum ./

# Download all dependencies needed to compile my application 
RUN go mod download

# Copy everything else into /app 
COPY . .

# Compile the Go code in the app directory 
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o main .

# Expose port 8081 to the outside world. This is for simplicity only, in a real-world scenario you would probably be more careful about exposing ports and handling things like HTTPS on your own! 
EXPOSE 8081

# Run the web service on container startup (the command below replaces the shell form entrypoint config)
CMD ["./main"]
